claim: People engage in bounded dishonesty just enough to benefit materially while maintaining a positive self-image through inattention and category-flexibility.
Physical tactile properties (weight, roughness, hardness) nonconsciously activate metaphorically-linked dimensions that shape evaluations of unrelated social targets.
Body cues, not facial expressions, carry diagnostic information for differentiating intense positive from negative affect; faces become congruent with body context through mimicry and perception.
Recent episodes of encoding or retrieval induce lasting biases that carry forward to shape how subsequent mnemonic decisions are made.
Analytic reasoning, when engaged either chronically or through priming, promotes skepticism toward religious belief compared to intuitive processing.
Costly punishment alone does not improve collective resource outcomes; its effectiveness depends critically on simultaneous communication to enable coordination.
Reading literary fiction temporarily enhances the ability to infer others' mental states more than reading popular fiction or expository text.
Agents automatically and continuously represent others' beliefs in real time, and these representations guide behavior even when the other agent is absent and their beliefs are task-irrelevant.
Physical cleansing (hand-washing) eliminates postdecisional dissonance by inducing a metaphorical 'clean slate' that allows people to disengage from prior choice justifications.
Repeated mental simulation of consuming a stimulus induces the same habituation effect (reduced desire) as actual consumption, demonstrating that mental representation alone suffices.
Testing improves long-term retention because test conditions generate mediators (linking information) that are both more retrievable and more likely to elicit correct responses.
Brief expressive writing about upcoming exam worries reduces cognitive interference from anxiety and improves exam performance, especially for chronically test-anxious students.
Having less (financial, temporal, or other resources) causes a shift in attention allocation that deepens focus on immediate problems while neglecting broader consequences.
When future access to information is assured, people encode location-of-information rather than the information itself, treating external systems as distributed memory.
Being alone with one's thoughts without external stimulation produces an aversive experience that many people find more unpleasant than mild physical discomfort.
People unconsciously substitute physical warmth for social warmth as a self-regulatory response to loneliness, without explicit awareness of this substitution mechanism.
Goals activated outside awareness effectively guide behavior with the same adaptive properties as conscious goal-pursuit, including adaptation to situational demands.

phenomenon: retroactive influence of future events on present response
claim: Future events exert anomalous retroactive influence on present cognitive and affective responses, with effects larger for individuals high in stimulus-seeking.

phenomenon: postural-embodiment of power and its physiological-behavioral consequences
claim: Adopting high-power body postures causally induces elevated testosterone, reduced cortisol, and increased risk-taking and feelings of efficacy.
Positive affect that is high in approach motivation narrows attentional focus, whereas low-approach positive affect broadens it; approach motivation modulates the breadth effect.
